title: Proclamation No. 329, s. 1938
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAIMATION NO. 329

RESERVING FOR AIRPLANE LANDING PURPOSES THREE PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE BARRIO OF SAN JOSE, MUNICIPALITY OF TACLOBAN, PROVINCE OF LEYTE, ISLAND OF LEYTE.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for airplane landing purposes, under the administration of the Bureau of Aeronautics, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following-described parcels of the public domain, situated in the barrio of San Jose, municipality of Tacloban, Province of Leyte, Island of Leyte, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plan Swo-15346, to wit:

Lot No. 5658. —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Swo-15346, N. 74° 30′ W. 661.07 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 3, thence S. 5° 01′ E. 83.53 m. to point 2; S. 1° 09′ W. 84.83 m. to point 3; S. 20° 15′ W. 18.21 m. to point 4; N. 61° 59′ W. 4.04 m. to point 5; N. 3° 57′ E. 81.78 m. to point 6; N. 0° 46′ W. 101.62 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 947 square meters.

Points 1, 5, and 6, points on water line; and the rest are B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.

Bounded on the east by lots Nos. 5662, 5649, and 5650; on the south, by lot No. 1771; and on the west, by Cancabato Bay.

Lot No. 5673. —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Swo-15346, N. 2° 10′ W. 1,020.86 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 3, thence S. 68° 20′ W. 445.34 m. to point 2; S. 15° 08′ W. 161.46 m. to point 3; S. 55° 34′ W. 50.04 m. to point 4; N. 13° 54′ E. 311.00 m. to point 5; N. 21° 11′ W, 229.78 m . to point 6; N. 19° 51′ W. 53.99 m. to point 7; N. 67° 59’ E. 80.19 m. to point 8; N. 77° 16 E., 260.20 m. to point 9; S. 4° 38′ E. 81.34 m. to point 10; S. 38° 40′ E. 50.86 m. to point 11; N. 48° 46’ E. 10.68 m. to point 12; N. 23° 18′ W. 13.85 m. to point 13; N. 32° 19’ E. 50.80 m. to point 14; N. 23° 03’ E. 55.92 m. to point 15; N. 74° to 40’ E. 112.17 m. to point 16; S 0° 25’ W. 328 m to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 176,754 square meters

Points 4, 5, and 6, points on water line; points 7 to 14. inclusive, stakes; point 15, galvanized iron spike on cross tree; and the rest are B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm

Bounded on the north by creek and lot No. 5674; on the east by lot No. 5674; on the southeast, by lots Nos. 5672 and 5671; and on the west, by Cancabato Bay.

Lot No. 5674. —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Swo-15346, S. 2° 07′ E. 55.63 m., more or less from B. L. L. M. No. 3, thence N. 0° 04′ E. 97 79 m. to point 2; N. 4° 48′ E. 144.29 m. to point 3; N. 8° 40′ W. 55.62 m. to point 4; N. 6° 49’ W. 69.03 m. to point 5; N. 12° 42′ E. 76.66 m. to point 6; N. 1° 35′ W. 122.55 m. to point 7; N. 0° 20′ W. 75.10 m. to point 8; N. 0° 32′ W. 60.47 m to point 9; N. 6° 27′ W. 69.83 m. to point 10; N. 3° 28’ W.

61.62 m. to point 11; N. 7° 54′ W. 60.50 m. to point 12; N. 1° 10′ W. 80.97 m. to point 13; N. 14° 19′ W. 109.38 m. to point 14; N. 0° 25′ E. 328.68 m. to point 15; S. 74° 40′ W. 112.17 m. to point 16; S. 85° 52′ W. 53.72 m. to point 17; S. 59° 14′ W. 8 37 m. to point 18; S. 88° 29′ E. 50.88 m to point 19; S. 29° 40′ W. 91.26 m. to point 20; N. 45° 51′ W. 26 01 m. to point 21; N. 7° 33′ W. 89.03 m. to point 22; S. 76° 43′ W. 275.51 m. to point 23; N. 55° 17′ W. 102.42 m. to point 24; N. 14° 37′ E. 89.04 m. to point 25; N. 77° 49’ E. 87.16 m to point 26; S. 57° 20′ E. 51.74 m. to point 27; N. 86° 08′ E. 148 36 m. to point 28; N. 88° 32′ E. 42.24 m to point 29; N. 38° 01′ E. 12.22 m. to point 30; N. 88° 30′ W. 4146 m. to point 31; S. 87° 22′ W. 155.01 m. to point 32 N. 50° 50’ W. 49.57 m. to point 33; S. 77° 08’ W. 103.10 m. to point 34; S. 15° 33′ W. 104.37 m. to point 35; S. 52° 43′ E 100.13 m. to point 36; S. 76° 39′ W. 75 03 m. to point 37; N. 47° 37′ W. 102.21 m. to point 38; N. 0° 30′ E. 128.02 m to point 39; N. 50° 14’ E. 96.30 m. to point 40; S. 76° 16′ E 172.69 m. to point 41; N. 84° 00′ E. 403 20 m. to point 42; S. 42° 32’ E. 191.58 m. to point 43; S. 5° 22′ E. 284.20 m to point 44; S. 0° 19’ W. 870.58 m. to point 45; S. 10° 10′ W. 270.30 m. to point 46; S. 84° 50’ W. 84.55 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 312,661 square meters.

Point 16, galvanized iron spike in cross on tree; points 17 to 37, inclusive, stakes; points 38 to 46, inclusive, points on water line; and the rest are B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.

Bounded on the north, by Cancabato Bay and San Pedro Bay; on the east, by San Pedro Bay; on the south, by lot No. 1746, lot No. 5673, and creek; and on the west, by lots Nos. 5652, 5653, 5654, 5656, 5659, 5663, 5664, 5665, 5666, 5670, 5669, 5671, 5672, 5673, creek and Cancabato Bay.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 15′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Swo-15346.

Surveyed: April 22-28 and September 27-28, 1926.

Approved: July 15, 1938.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this eighth day of October, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-eight, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the third.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President

Sources: Presidential Museum and Library
